original: When enabled, the difficulty level for combat is greatly reduced.
When enabled, some text will change to fit a sillier tone.
When enabled, a prompt will appear when you save over a save slot with existing saved data.
When enabled, the option to add and display notes will be available when saving to a save slot in the data menu.
When enabled, a prompt will appear when you load a save slot’s data over your current game’s progress.
When enabled, a prompt will appear before making a final transaction with a vendor. (This option also applies to the inventory drop menu.)
When enabled, each time the player moves through their own volition, the move will be undoable, back to an earlier state in the game.
Limits the number of available Undo states to keep records of.
When enabled, the game will automatically display the helper to explain any new-game experience the player encounters. Players who no longer wish to see these prompts can turn this feature off.

Sets the automatic-scroll position each time a new action is displayed in the combat log during a turn. Choose between the first new card or last new card of the current turn, or the very bottom of the page.
Customize the deck of playing cards used for certain minigames.

Configure automatic submission of error logs. Disabled will completely disable the system. Automatic will send errors when they are generated. Prompt will require user input on the error screen to send the error.
Configure periodic update checks that will notify you if a game update is available.

When enabled, author attributions will be visible for scenes, where applicable.
When enabled, the damage and stat-change numbers will be color-coded upon output.
When enabled, event logs will display inline with normal text instead of displaying as a separate scene.
When enabled, event logs will have timestamps that are color-coded upon output.
Define the font size of the main content text.
Define the line height of the main content text.
When disabled, enemy combat descriptions only appear on the first round.
When enabled, printed damage values (Shield, Health, Lust) will be combined into one value.
When enabled, the player character’s
stat (genital configuration) will be replaced with a
stat (gender alignment).
When enabled, the non-player character’s
stat (genital configuration) will be replaced with a
stat (gender alignment).
Adjust display of units of length.
Adjust display of units of volume.
Adjust display of units of liquid.
Adjust display of units of weight.
When enabled, the date’s year output will be in the A.C. (After Confederation) format, otherwise it will retain the “XX”-year format.
When enabled, the clock output will be in the 12-hour AM/PM format.
When enabled, item tooltips will display the full stat details of an item outside of the Inspect/Compare view, where applicable.
Adjust the visibility of the keybind control indicators. A 0% visibility value will hide the keybind indicators entirely.
When enabled,
. The micromap is an even more condensed version of the collapsed minimap with no overlapping controls.
When enabled, the minimap and micromap will display slightly larger map tiles in the sidebar.
When enabled, if the minimap is rolled out, the bust artwork will automatically roll out to replace it when navigation is not available.
Change the position of the bust and map when rolled out.
When enabled, notifications for events that cause significant reputation changes will be visible, if applicable.
When enabled, the game will have a grayscale color filter.
When enabled, a displayed bust that has multiple artists will try to match its base bust’s assigned artist. For consistent results, most if not all artist override settings should be cleared.
Adjust the visibility of the bust control buttons.
When enabled, scene-related images will be displayed inline alongside the text, if available.
When enabled, scene-related videos will be displayed inline alongside the text, if available.
When enabled, videos will automatically play after they are loaded.
When enabled, videos will loop indefinitely when they are played.
Determine whether the displayed image will be crisp (pixelate) or be blurred (smooth) when rendered scaled up or down from its original size.
When enabled, scanline graphics and other backdrop elements will be rendered as opaque instead of semi-transparent. This may help with increasing performance on older devices.
When enabled, the mannequin bust art will be rendered as opaque instead of semi-transparent. This may help with increasing performance on older devices.
When enabled, special effects related to map tiles will be visible.
When enabled, special effects related to map foreground and background will be visible.

When enabled, vector images with unique filter effects, such as glowing edges on strokes, will be visible. Disabling this may help with increasing performance on older devices, or may correct visible rendering issues caused by devices that do not fully support SVG filter blocks.

When enabled, vector images with unique morphing transitions, will be animated. Disabling this may help with increasing performance on older devices, or may correct visible rendering issues caused by devices that do not fully support SVG morphing transitions.
Choose and apply color theme presets for the game.
When enabled, there will be a smooth animated transition during auto scrolling to a target in the text area.
When enabled, text will animate, where available.
When enabled, contextual button notifications will be animated.
When enabled, scanline graphics will be animated.
When enabled, vector images and icons will be animated.
When enabled, inline images will be animated, where available.
Adjust the settings for the mannequin animations.
When enabled, the image/map viewer will animate when transitioning between view modes.
Increase the player’s XP to next level.
Unlock all ship decoration items in the decoration inventory.
Unlock all panty key items.
Unlock all VR programs.
Add Zil Rations to your inventory.
Use Doctor Badger’s cheat menu.
When enabled, the classic Lapinara will appear on Tarkus.
Use Doctor Illustria Po’s cheat menu.
When enabled, Ardia will still display her sex menu at high DM.
Spawn Kattom Osgood in the current room.
Spawn Bianca in the current room.
Unlocks Mitzi.
Unlock the Shukuchi encounter.
Reset Akane content.
Reset Bizzy content.
Reset Delilah content.
Reset Jasvalla to before you meet.
Reset Narc’s dungeon.
Reset Nastizia’s quest to pre-invite.
Reset Tainted Rusher content.
Reset the Warg’ii Hold questline.
Enabling viewing all images in the gallery without first unlocking each image.

Configure how the minimap responds to input. Autopathing allows you to touch/click a specific room and the game will calculate a route to take you to that room. Directional allows you to touch areas of the map to move in specific directions.
Specify the delay time in milliseconds between each room movement undertaken by autopathing.

When enabled, the sidebar will change to the legacy layout scheme. This layout will incorporate large buttons and add a Map/Stats toggle button to the sidebar. Combat and Passive stats will display depending on the position of the map.

Enabling the “Pre-Legacy Style” will split the interface into a two-sidebar layout for some UI states, forgoing the Map/Stats toggle and converting roll-out elements into overlapping pop-up elements instead.
When enabled, instead of toggling the minimap roll-out window, the full map of the area will be displayed instead.
When enabled, while actively navigating with the full map open, any scene that appears and prevents navigation will automatically close the map to readily view the scene.
